agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have issued directly conflicting, non-negotiable requirements about whether courier GPS location should be visible to customers.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Courier Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"A delivery driver who picks up and transports packages to customers."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's current GPS coordinates, updated continuously during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery","definition":"The active fulfillment phase from pickup to drop-off during which tracking would be relevant."},{"term":"Customer","definition":"The end recipient of a package who may want visibility into delivery progress."},{"term":"Courier visibility","definition":"The degree to which a courier's location or progress is exposed to the customer during delivery."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"During an active delivery, the customer must receive some form of delivery progress information.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The form of progress information is a business policy decision — GPS coordinates, zone-level proximity, ETA, or status updates are all valid implementations. A VP can change the granularity.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery is in progress, when a customer views tracking, then at least one form of progress indicator is displayed."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Courier precise GPS coordinates must not be exposed directly to customers without courier consent or an approved privacy framework.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Exposing a worker's exact real-time location to third parties without a privacy framework creates legal liability (GDPR, labor law), violates worker dignity expectations, and if violated, cannot be un-done — the location data has been disclosed.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery is in progress, when a customer views tracking, then the raw GPS latitude/longitude of the courier is never transmitted to or rendered on the customer's device."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Courier retention concerns must be factored into any tracking feature design.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a workforce policy consideration. How it is balanced against customer experience is a strategic business decision.","testable_assertion":"Given a tracking feature is deployed, when courier satisfaction is measured, then the feature does not cause a statistically significant increase in courier attrition."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Customers must have a meaningful way to know when their delivery will arrive.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy — the mechanism (ETA, status updates, proximity zones) is a product decision.","testable_assertion":"Given a delivery is in progress, when a customer checks delivery status, then an estimated arrival time or proximity indicator is displayed."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A delivery is in active transit","when":"The customer opens the tracking view","then":"The system displays delivery progress without revealing the courier's exact GPS coordinates"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A delivery is in active transit","when":"The system renders a map or progress indicator","then":"The courier's position is abstracted (e.g., zone-level, street-level radius, or ETA-only) so exact coordinates are never exposed to the client"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A delivery is in active transit","when":"The API responds to a customer tracking request","then":"The response payload does not contain raw latitude/longitude fields for the courier"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"No active delivery exists for the customer","when":"The customer opens the tracking view","then":"No courier location or progress data is displayed"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A delivery has been completed","when":"The customer views the delivery record","then":"Historical courier location data is not accessible to the customer"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for an extended period (e.g., stuck in traffic)","expected_behavior":"System should show an updated ETA or status message rather than a static pin that reveals the courier is not moving, which could prompt customer complaints or harassment."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates significantly from expected route","expected_behavior":"System should still show only ETA or zone-level proximity; route deviations must not be visible to the customer."},{"scenario":"Multiple deliveries on the same courier route","expected_behavior":"Each customer sees only their own delivery progress; no information about other stops or sequencing is leaked."},{"scenario":"GPS signal loss during delivery","expected_behavior":"System shows last known status with a staleness indicator (e.g., 'Updated 3 min ago') rather than a stale precise location."},{"scenario":"Customer screenshots or records tracking data","expected_behavior":"Even captured data should not contain exact coordinates, since the abstraction happens server-side before transmission."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Getting this wrong in either direction is costly. Losing couriers (Operations concern) increases recruitment and training costs — typically $3K-$5K per driver. Losing customers due to poor transparency (Customer Success concern) reduces lifetime value. The resolution must balance both cost centers.","operational":"Courier tracking infrastructure must be built regardless — Operations needs it for dispatch, routing, and SLA monitoring. The question is purely about what is exposed to the customer. Abstraction layers add modest complexity but protect the business from both stakeholder concerns.","user":"Customers get meaningful delivery visibility (ETA, proximity) which research consistently shows is the actual driver of satisfaction — not raw GPS. Couriers get privacy protection, improving retention and trust.","risk":"HIGH risk if exact GPS is shipped: labor law exposure (worker surveillance without consent framework), GDPR/privacy regulation violations in many jurisdictions, courier attrition. MODERATE risk if no tracking at all: competitive disadvantage, customer churn. LOW risk with abstracted tracking: satisfies both stakeholder intents without the downsides.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Receive delivery progress visibility through abstracted means (ETA, proximity zones). Satisfies the core need for transparency without exact coordinates."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Protected from real-time surveillance by customers. Reduces feeling of being monitored, addressing retention concern."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Must accept that 'exact GPS coordinates' is not the correct implementation of their goal. Their real goal — transparency as a differentiator — is achievable through abstracted tracking."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Their hard constraint (no exact GPS to customers) is preserved. Internal dispatch systems can still use precise GPS."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Exposing worker GPS to third parties has regulatory implications that neither team has addressed. This stakeholder is missing from the conversation."},{"group":"Product Leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict and approve the abstraction approach. This decision cannot be made by either team unilaterally."}]},"confidence":{"level":42,"reasoning":"Two explicit, non-negotiable stakeholder requirements directly contradict each other. Neither team has authority to override the other. While a domain-informed resolution path exists (abstracted tracking), the actual business decision has not been made. Confidence is low because the spec is fundamentally conflicted and no arbiter has ruled.","high_confidence_areas":["Raw GPS coordinates should never be sent to customer clients — this is a privacy and labor-law baseline","Customers need some form of delivery progress visibility","An abstraction layer (ETA, proximity zones) is the standard industry resolution to this exact tension","Internal operations systems should retain full GPS precision for dispatch and routing"],"low_confidence_areas":["What level of abstraction is acceptable to Customer Success (street-level? zone-level? ETA-only?)","Whether couriers should have opt-in control over their visibility level","Regulatory requirements for the specific jurisdictions this operates in","Whether Product Leadership has already taken a position on this"],"assumptions":["Neither team has executive override authority over the other","The company operates in jurisdictions where worker location privacy is legally relevant","Customer Success's real goal is delivery transparency, not specifically raw GPS coordinates","Operations' concern is genuine and based on measurable attrition data"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact GPS coordinates to customers as non-negotiable. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers as non-negotiable. These two requirements are mutually exclusive and both teams claim their position is non-negotiable.","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Success","Operations","Couriers","Customers","Legal/Compliance","Product Leadership"],"options":["A: Ship exact GPS to customers (satisfies Customer Success, violates Operations, creates legal risk, accelerates courier attrition)","B: Ship no tracking at all (satisfies Operations, violates Customer Success, competitive disadvantage)","C: Ship abstracted tracking — ETA and proximity zones — which satisfies the INTENT of both teams without satisfying the LITERAL requirement of either (recommended industry-standard approach)"],"recommendation":"Option C. Both teams are expressing legitimate business needs but have over-specified their solutions. Customer Success needs transparency — not raw GPS. Operations needs courier privacy — not zero tracking. Abstracted tracking (ETA, proximity zones, status updates) is the proven industry pattern used by every major logistics company. Escalate to Product Leadership to arbitrate and adopt this middle path. Additionally, involve Legal to establish a courier data privacy framework before any tracking ships."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Shipping exact courier GPS coordinates to customers","business_rule_broken":"BR-2 — Courier precise GPS must not be exposed without privacy framework. Also violates Operations' documented driver retention concern and creates unaddressed legal/regulatory exposure."},{"violation":"Shipping zero delivery tracking to customers","business_rule_broken":"BR-1 and BR-4 — Customers must receive delivery progress information. Zero tracking fails the transparency requirement and creates competitive disadvantage."}]}

Abstracted tracking (ETA, proximity zones, status updates) is the proven industry pattern used by every major logistics company. Escalate to Product Leadership to arbitrate and adopt this middle path. Additionally, involve Legal to establish a courier data privacy framework before any tracking ships."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Shipping exact courier GPS coordinates to customers","business_rule_broken":"BR-2 — Courier precise GPS must not be exposed without privacy framework.
